db_integrity.notes: Autoresearch proof-build-only did not complete within the measurement cap, so no new large completed DB integrity result exists for this phase.

comprehensive_benchmark.notes: The comprehensive benchmark intentionally still uses the persisted Autoresearch cold-build artifact and keeps the cold-build/storage failures visible.

modes_confirmed.purpose: Production proof DB build path. Excludes storage audit, relation sampler, CGC comparison, manual label processing, repeated DB rebuild, and full markdown report generation.
Proof DB build plus full integrity validation.

modes_confirmed.command: full gate or explicit audit/debug sidecar build
modes_confirmed.purpose: Separate audit/debug sidecar work. Not counted as production proof-build-only.
Aggregated correctness, context, storage, indexing, latency, and comparison-readiness reporting. Not the production cold-build target.

implementation_changes: Added explicit index build modes and CLI parsing for proof-build-only and proof-build-plus-validation.
Cold indexes build into a hidden temp DB and publish only after the configured gate passes.
Proof-build-only uses hidden-temp bulk-load pragmas during the temp build, then restores durable WAL/FULL settings before publish.
Proof-build-only skips the in-transaction quick_check and relies on hidden-temp and final publish quick checks after commit.
Proof-build-plus-validation keeps the full integrity gate.
Proof-build-only fast bulk finish avoids ANALYZE and WAL checkpoint work in the build phase.
Template entity and edge writes use prepared statements and a store-local dictionary cache.

remaining_bottlenecks.stage: template and dictionary persistence inside large batches
remaining_bottlenecks.evidence: After the fast-temp changes, batch 1 still spent 81.335s in db_write_ms for 51,240 entities and 41,263 edges.
Batches with tens of thousands of template entities dominate write time; small batches complete in tens of milliseconds.

remaining_bottlenecks.stage: per-row dictionary and template table writes
remaining_bottlenecks.evidence: Fixture profile shows dictionary lookup/insert remains the dominant profiled insert span even on a tiny fixture.
The system still writes hundreds of thousands of template rows during cold proof build rather than bulk-loading from compact columnar buffers.
No longer the primary suspect for the timeout, but still separated so validation/audit are not counted as production proof-build-only.

storage_change_safety_questions.did_proof_db_size_decrease: not measured as a completed Autoresearch DB in this phase; no storage optimization was claimed
storage_change_safety_questions.removed_data_destination: no proof data was removed; this phase changed build-time durability/profile behavior only

next_required_work: Split template/dictionary persistence profile further inside db_write_ms for completed large batches.
Replace per-row dictionary lookup/insert with batch interning for symbols, qname prefixes, qualified names, and provenance IDs.
Bulk-load template_entities and template_edges through temporary staging tables or prepared multi-row chunks.
Keep validation/audit/reporting out of proof-build-only measurements.
